2026-06-18T18:55:53.4936097Z [36;1mset -euo pipefail[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4936495Z [36;1m# actions/download-artifact (>=v5) flattens contents directly into `path/`[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4937391Z [36;1m# whenever exactly one artifact matches the pattern, ignoring[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4937866Z [36;1m# `merge-multiple: false`. Re-shape into the per-group subdir layout so[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4938368Z [36;1m# downstream validation sees a stable structure regardless of matrix size.[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4938768Z [36;1m# Upstream reference:[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4939195Z [36;1m# https://github.com/actions/download-artifact/blob/v8.0.1/src/download-artifact.ts[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4939756Z [36;1m# (see the `isSingleArtifactDownload || mergeMultiple || artifacts.length === 1`[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4940276Z [36;1m# branch). Remove this step once download-artifact stops flattening or[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4940685Z [36;1m# exposes an opt-out.[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4941036Z [36;1mexpected_count=$(echo "$EXPECTED_MATRIX" | jq '.group // [] | length')[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4941430Z [36;1mif [ "$expected_count" -eq 1 ]; then[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4941777Z [36;1m  group_id=$(echo "$EXPECTED_MATRIX" | jq -r '.group[0].id')[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4942235Z [36;1m  # Defence-in-depth: group_id is interpolated into a shell path. apm-prep[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4942724Z [36;1m  # produces a sanitised id today, but enforce a strict allowlist here so[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4943181Z [36;1m  # any future schema drift cannot smuggle traversal sequences.[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4943617Z [36;1m  if ! printf '%s' "$group_id" | grep -Eq '^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$'; then[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4944054Z [36;1m    echo "::error::unsafe group_id '$group_id' (must match ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$)"[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4944414Z [36;1m    exit 1[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4944627Z [36;1m  fi[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4944940Z [36;1m  group_dir="/tmp/gh-aw/apm-bundles/${ARTIFACT_PREFIX}apm-${group_id}"[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4945309Z [36;1m  if [ ! -d "$group_dir" ]; then[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4945797Z [36;1m    mkdir -p "$group_dir"[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4946262Z [36;1m    find /tmp/gh-aw/apm-bundles -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -path "$group_dir" -exec mv {} "$group_dir/" \;[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4946721Z [36;1m  fi[0m
2026-06-18T18:55:53.4946916Z [36;1mfi[0m

2026-06-18T18:56:13.2946619Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-06-18T18:56:13.2946882Z [36;1m[0m

In practice this is unreachable through the builder because _require_str() in yml_schema.py rejects empty or whitespace-only names at YAML load time. However, build_tag_regex is a public API (exported in __all__) and a future caller passing name='' would unknowingly restore the monorepo cross-package tag-bleeding behavior this PR was written to fix. 2026-06-18T19:04:39.9541852Z   "findings": [
2026-06-18T19:04:39.9542434Z     {
2026-06-18T19:04:39.9671749Z       "severity": "recommended",
2026-06-18T19:04:41.0595193Z       "summary": "version_resolver.resolve_version_constraint bypasses the name= kwarg via manual str.replace, creating a silent divergence risk",
2026-06-18T19:04:49.0146898Z       "rationale": "resolve_version_constraint (version_resolver.py:80-81) does pinned_pattern = tag_pattern.replace('{name}', plugin_name) then calls build_tag_regex(pinned_pattern) with no name= arg. This pre-substitutes the name manually rather than routing through the canonical kwarg the PR just proved is correct. If build_tag_regex ever evolves its name handling -- empty-name guard, re.escape edge cases, error paths -- this call site silently diverges without a test to catch it. All other external callers (tag_pattern.py:138, tag_pattern.py:164) already use the name= kwarg form. The PR notes the inconsistency in its Trade-offs section and defers it; given the fix proves name= is the right idiom and the change is one line in one function, standardising now costs less than a deferred cleanup and eliminates the divergence window permanently.\n\nDesign patterns -- Used in this PR: none -- straight-line procedural bug fix (missing argument), appropriate for this scope. 2026-06-18T19:14:02.2813806Z   "arbitration": "The PR delivers a precise, well-scoped correctness fix: a single missing kwarg (`name=entry.name`) in `builder.py:_resolve_version_range` caused `build_tag_regex` to compile `{name}` as the wildcard `[^/]+`, pooling all monorepo packages into one global tag set and silently handing every entry the highest version in the repository. 2026-06-18T19:14:26.6410551Z       "from_persona": "supply-chain-security-expert",

2026-06-18T19:14:28.8499430Z       "why": "`build_tag_regex` is a public API. A future caller passing `name=''` would unknowingly restore the exact monorepo tag-bleeding behavior this PR fixes. A one-line comment prevents rediscovering the bug from outside the builder call-site.",

2026-06-18T19:20:40.4831635Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4831916Z [163] ✓ view(path: /home/runner/work/apm/apm/src/apm_cli/co..., view_range: [1030, 1110])
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4832219Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4832549Z [164] ◐ Now I have a thorough understanding of the PR. Let me assess it from a DevX/UX perspective:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4832969Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4834030Z   1. **The fix itself**: The one-line change (`build_tag_regex(pattern, name=entry.name)`) correctly restores the user promise that "each package resolves its own version." This is the core UX fix - in a monorepo, each package should see only its own tags. Before this fix, all packages competed in the same tag pool.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4835060Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4835470Z   2. **The inconsistency between `_resolve_version_range` and `version_resolver.resolve_version_constraint`**:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4836099Z      - `_resolve_version_range` now uses: `build_tag_regex(pattern, name=entry.name)` (after the fix)
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4837452Z      - `version_resolver.resolve_version_constraint` handles the same logic differently by pre-substituting the `{name}` placeholder with the plugin name before calling `build_tag_regex`, which achieves the same functional result since the placeholder is already resolved.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4838730Z      - This inconsistency isn't really a user-facing bug, but rather a code organization issue that the PR author flagged as technical debt to address later.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4839728Z      - Looking at the `NoMatchingVersionError` message, it formats the error with the version range, package name, and additional context like the pattern and remote repository...
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4841141Z      - The problem is that while the error does show the pattern, it displays the raw template (like `{name}-v{version}`) rather than the actual substituted value (like `pkg-a-v{version}`), which could confuse users about whether the variable expansion is working correctly.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4842056Z      
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4843335Z      This is worth flagging as a minor UX improvement—not a blocker, but something that would help with debugging. The real concern now is whether there's a risk of inconsistency across different code paths, particularly between how `_resolve_version_range` in `builder.py` handles this versus other resolution methods.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4844409Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4844820Z   Let me trace where `resolve_version_constraint` gets called to see if there's a genuine split-brain scenario here.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4845213Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4845649Z [165] ✓ $ grep -n "resolve_version_constraint\|from.*version_resolver" /home/runner/work/apm/apm/src/apm_cli/marketplace/*.py
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4846075Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4846659Z [166] ◐ Now let me look at more of the PackageEntry schema to understand if `name` can be empty/None, and how `build_tag_regex` handles the case where `name` is empty string or None.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4847458Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4847673Z   Looking at the key parts:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4847905Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4848362Z   1. **PR Diff**: The fix is in `builder.py` line 719: `tag_rx = build_tag_regex(pattern, name=entry.name)` (was `build_tag_regex(pattern)`)
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4848877Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4849129Z   2. **`build_tag_regex` implementation** in `tag_pattern.py`:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4849454Z   ```python
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4849773Z   name_rx = re.escape(name) if _PLACEHOLDER_NAME in pattern and name else r"[^/]+"
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4850142Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4850314Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4850491Z   ```
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4851067Z   When `name` is provided AND the pattern contains `{name}`, it escapes the name for regex. Otherwise it falls back to a wildcard that matches anything except forward slashes.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4851692Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4852381Z   3. **`PackageEntry.name`**: It's typed as `str` (not `str | None`), so it shouldn't be None. But if `entry.name` is an empty string, it's falsy, so the regex would fall back to the wildcard pattern instead of using the escaped name.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4853119Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4854577Z   4. **Security perspective**: The old behavior without the `name=` parameter treated `{name}` as a wildcard, allowing tags from any package in the same monorepo to match. This creates a dependency confusion vulnerability within the monorepo—if a malicious or misconfigured package had a higher version number, it would be resolved for all other packages, which is a real supply-chain risk.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4855841Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4856111Z   5. **`re.escape` analysis**: Still examining how the escaping works...
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4856561Z   ```
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4856738Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4856927Z   python
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4857451Z   temp = pattern.replace(_PLACEHOLDER_VERSION, _sentinel_version)
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4857861Z   temp = temp.replace(_PLACEHOLDER_NAME, _sentinel_name)
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4858191Z   escaped = re.escape(temp)
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4858530Z   escaped = escaped.replace(re.escape(_sentinel_version), _VERSION_RX)
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4858961Z   escaped = escaped.replace(re.escape(_sentinel_name), name_rx)
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4859276Z   ```
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4859466Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4860689Z   The sentinel strategy avoids edge cases by replacing placeholders with unique markers before escaping, then swapping them back with the properly escaped values. This prevents regex injection from malicious package names while keeping the regex patterns intact. the escaped null bytes should match correctly during replacement. For the e... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2695 chars]
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4861972Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4862272Z [167] ✓ view(path: /home/runner/work/apm/apm/src/apm_cli/ma..., view_range: [1, 80])

2026-06-18T19:20:40.4920008Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4920288Z [200] ✓ view(path: /home/runner/work/apm/apm/src/apm_cli/co..., view_range: [450, 600])
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4920588Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4920768Z [201] ◐ Now I have a clear picture. Let me analyze:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4921055Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4922627Z   1. `resolve_version_constraint` in `version_resolver.py` is called from `resolver.py` for `apm install` style version resolution (per-plugin from a marketplace). This is for single-plugin resolution, where the caller passes `plugin_name` directly, and the pattern does `tag_pattern.replace("{name}", plugin_name)` before calling `build_tag_regex`. Since `{name}` is already replaced, the pattern passed to `build_tag_regex` has no `{name}` placeholder, so `build_tag_regex` can't fall into the wildcard path.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4924212Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4924487Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4926203Z   2. `_resolve_version_range` in `builder.py` handles `apm pack` (marketplace building), where it resolves all packages at once. The bug was that calling `build_tag_regex(pattern)` without passing `name=entry.name` would leave `{name}` as a wildcard, causing all packages to compete in the same tag pool. These two functions serve different commands—one for `apm install` and one for `apm pack`—and the inconsistency is purely in how they invoke the regex builder.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4927890Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4929058Z   Looking at the error message quality, the current `NoMatchingVersionError` from `_resolve_version_range` shows the raw pattern string like `{name}-v{version}` instead of the effective pattern for that specific package. This creates a minor UX gap where users have to mentally substitute the placeholder themselves rather than seeing the actual pattern that was used.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4930264Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4931489Z   The same issue appears in `_resolver.py` where the error detail also includes the raw pattern with the `{name}` placeholder. Since the fix correctly scopes the pattern, showing the unsubstituted version is confusing—users might wonder if the placeholder was actually expanded or if that's the root cause of their problem.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4932580Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4933020Z   I need to check how this error gets surfaced to the user in the CLI to understand if improving the error message is worth the effort.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4933453Z

2026-06-18T19:20:40.4946066Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4946699Z   Looking at the PR diff, the new tests include `test_two_packages_resolve_independently` with `>=1.0.0` and build-level `{name}-v{version}` patterns, plus `test_two_packages_double_dash_pattern`...
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4947545Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4949112Z   The new tests cover the double-dash variant with `{name}--v{version}`, per-entry `tag_pattern` scoping, and the `^1.0.0` constraint with no matching tags scenario. But `version_resolver.resolve_version_constraint` uses a different `str.replace` approach that replaces `{name}` directly rather than using a `name=` kwarg - this is a known trade-off mentioned in the PR. That code path isn't part of this fix, though there's existing test coverage like `test_ignores_other_plugin_tags` in the version resolver tests.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4950738Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4951402Z   One gap I notice is special characters in package names - the PR mentions `re.escape` handles them safely, but there's no explicit test for a name like `my.pkg` that contains regex-special characters. None:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4952240Z       result = render_tag("{name}-v{version}", name="my.tool", version="1.0.0")
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4952616Z       assert result == "my.tool-v1.0.0"
2026-06-18T19:20:40.4952875Z   ```

2026-06-18T19:20:40.5013118Z [229] ◐ Now I have a complete picture. Let me synthesize my findings:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5013449Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5013663Z   ### Findings:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5013874Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5014168Z   **1. The fix is correct and restores the core user promise (no issues)**
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5014507Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5015121Z   The one-line change correctly scopes each package's tag resolution to its own name. Before the fix, a user could run `apm pack` with a monorepo pattern like `{name}-v{version}` and see:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5015778Z   ```
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5016054Z   pkg-a resolved to: pkg-b-v2.0.0  # wrong! competing in same pool
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5016426Z   pkg-b resolved to: pkg-b-v2.0.0  # correct
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5016695Z   ```
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5017172Z   After the fix, each package resolves its own highest tag. This is exactly what a `npm`/`cargo`-style user expects.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5017627Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5017805Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5018143Z   **2. The deferred inconsistency is cosmetically inconsistent but NOT a split-brain risk**
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5018547Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5019647Z   The two code paths handle tag pattern resolution slightly differently—one explicitly passes the package name to the regex builder while the other substitutes it into the pattern first—but both arrive at the same correct result since the `{name}` placeholder gets resolved either way.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5020620Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5021802Z   There's a nuance in how `build_tag_regex` checks for the placeholder: it looks for `{name}` in the pattern, but in the `version_resolver.py` path that placeholder has already been replaced by string substitution, so the condition evaluates to false and the code uses a generic fallback pattern instead. Since there's no placeholder left to process, this doesn't create any actual risk.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5023029Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5025198Z   The real issue is that the error message displays the raw pattern with `{name}` as a placeholder, which could confuse users trying to debug why their version resolution failed.  A more helpful approach would be to show the effective pattern after substitution—like `pkg-a-v{version}` instead of `{name}-v{version}`—so users can immediately see what tag pattern the system is actually looking for. The generic hint about checking version ranges doesn't address this either, so including the effective pattern in the error detail would make it much more actionable.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5027062Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5027552Z   I'm realizing this is really a UX gap rather than a blocking issue. The PR itself fixes the core corr... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 3500 chars]

2026-06-18T19:20:40.5029186Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5029940Z [232] ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/apm/apm && pip install -e ".[dev]" --quiet 2>&1 | tail -5 && python -m pytest tests/unit/marketplace/test_builder.py::TestMonorepoNameScopedTagPattern -v --tb=short 2>&1
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5030627Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5030977Z [233] ◐ Now I have all the information I need to write the security review. Let me analyze all the findings:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5031414Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5032086Z   1. **The bug (pre-fix) and its supply-chain security implications**: The old code `build_tag_regex(pattern)` without `name=entry.name` caused `{name}` in patterns to be treated as `[^/]+` wildcard. This means:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5033079Z      - In a monorepo, ALL packages sharing the same git repo but with different tag prefixes would compete in the same tag pool.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5033822Z      - The package with the highest semver tag (regardless of which package it belongs to) would be selected for ALL entries.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5034918Z      - A malicious actor controlling any package's tags could push a high-version tag like `evil-pkg-v999.0.0` and have it resolve for other packages' version ranges, effectively hijacking their dependencies through tag-namespace pollution.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5035766Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5036476Z   2. **The fix's correctness**: The updated code now passes the package name to `build_tag_regex`, which properly escapes it and substitutes it into the pattern, ensuring each package's tags are isolated in their own namespace.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5037312Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5038035Z   3. **Runtime safety**: The `_require_str` validator ensures `entry.name` is always a non-empty string by the time version resolution happens, so the guard in `build_tag_regex` prevents any edge cases with missing or invalid names.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5038814Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5039777Z   4. **Fallback behavior**: If the pattern doesn't include `{name}` (like `"v{version}"`), the escaping has no effect since there's no name component to filter on anyway. If somehow `entry.name` were empty despite validation, the code silently falls back to the wildcard pattern, maintaining backward compatibility.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5040786Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5041146Z   5. **Attack surface**: This vulnerability only manifests in monorepo scenarios where patterns...
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5041559Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5042324Z   contain `{name}`, and an attacker would need write access to the repository to push malicious tags. In CI environments where contributors can push tags or third-party actions touch the tag namespace, this becomes a more realistic threat.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5043137Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5043586Z   6. **Consistency with existing code**: The `infer_tag_pattern` and `parse_tag_version`... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 4286 chars]
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5044021Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5044361Z [234] ✓ $ find /home/runner/work/apm/apm -name "panelist-return-schema.json" 2>/dev/null | head -5
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5044702Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5045806Z [235] ✓ $ python3 -c "import re; name = 'pkg.with.dots'; sentinel_name = '\x00NAME\x00'; temp = sentinel_name; escaped = re.escape(temp); name_rx = re.escape(name); escaped2 = escaped.replace(re.escape(sentinel_name), name_rx); print('escaped sentinel:', repr(escaped)); print('name_rx:', repr(name_rx)); print...
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5046736Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5047824Z [236] ◐ The `re.escape` sentinel strategy works correctly. The sentinel `\x00NAME\x00` uses null bytes which are preserved by `re.escape` (since `\x00` is non-alphanumeric, it gets escaped, but then `re.escape(_sentinel_name)` in the replace call produces the same escaped form so the replacement works).
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5048811Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5049613Z   For a package named `pkg.with.dots`, `re.escape` produces `pkg\.with\.dots` in the final regex, meaning it matches literally `pkg.with.dots` and NOT `pkg_with_dots`. This is the correct, safe behavior - the dots in the name are treated as literal characters.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5050464Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5050627Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5050962Z   Now I need to check the panelist-return-schema to see what the exact format looks like.

2026-06-18T19:20:40.5066397Z [239] ✓ $ which python3 python pypy3 2>/dev/null; python3 --version 2>/dev/null; python --version 2>/dev/null
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5066771Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5067259Z [240] ◐ Now I have a comprehensive picture of the codebase. Let me analyze the PR from a CLI logging/output UX perspective:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5067762Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5068813Z   1. **The bug**: `build_tag_regex(pattern)` was called without `name=entry.name` when pattern contains `{name}`. This made `{name}` a wildcard `[^/]+` instead of matching only the specific package's name. So if you had `pkg-a` and `pkg-b`, both packages would match tags from either package (e.g., `pkg-b-v2.0.0` matched the wildcard pattern for `pkg-a`).
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5069920Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5070088Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5070442Z   2. **The fix**: Pass `name=entry.name` to `build_tag_regex` so each package only matches its own tags.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5071562Z   3. **From CLI logging/UX perspective**: In a monorepo with `pkg-a` at v1.0.0 and `pkg-b` at v2.0.0 using the pattern `{name}-v{version}`, the bug would cause both packages to resolve to the highest global tag (`pkg-b-v2.0.0`), giving users incorrect version information in the success output.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5072560Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5073764Z   The real problem is that this silent wrong-version success happens without any warning—the user sees green success but the marketplace.json is corrupted. A verbose debug log showing which regex was compiled and which tags were considered as candidates would have immediately exposed this cross-contamination issue.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5074817Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5075466Z   The error message for `NoMatchingVersionError` is now more likely to fire correctly after the fix, displaying the pattern, version range, and package name to help users understand what went wrong.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5076171Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5077256Z   The hint currently says "Check that your version range matches published tags," but for monorepo users this isn't specific enough—it should explicitly mention that name-scoped tags might be missing and show an example of what the expected tag format should look like.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5078186Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5078857Z   The `_render_build_table` function already displays the correct information; after the fix, `pkg.ref` will now show the right package reference instead of pulling data from the wrong package due to the bug.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5079588Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5080321Z   The real issue is that the bug was invisible during normal operation because there was no diagnostic output to surface it—the fix ensures ... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 4695 chars]
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5080907Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5081215Z [241] ✓ glob(pattern: **/panelist-return-schema.json, paths: /home/runner/work/apm/apm)

2026-06-18T19:20:40.5081818Z [242] ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/apm/apm && pip3 install pytest pyyaml 2>&1 | tail -5

2026-06-18T19:20:40.5082574Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5083147Z [244] ◐ Now I have the full schema. Let me also verify the re.escape sentinel strategy one more time by running through the actual code path with a special character name.

2026-06-18T19:20:40.5101904Z [251] ◐ The verification confirms:
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5102155Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5102636Z   1. **Basic monorepo scoping**: After the fix, `pkg-a`'s regex no longer matches `pkg-b`'s tags, and vice versa. This confirms the fix is correct.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5103171Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5103651Z   2. **Wildcard (old behavior)**: Without `name=`, both `pkg-a-v1.0.0` and `pkg-b-v2.0.0` match the wildcard pattern - confirming the original bug.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5104169Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5104887Z   3. **Regex injection prevention**: `re.escape` correctly handles names with regex metacharacters like `.*`. The name `.*` is escaped to `\.\*`, so it only matches literal `.*` tags, not arbitrary strings. No regex injection possible.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5105656Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5105829Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5106326Z   4. **Dots in name**: Names with dots like `my.pkg` get properly escaped so they match the exact tag `my.pkg-v1.0.0` but not variations like `mypkg-v1.0.0`.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5108074Z   5. **Empty name fallback**: When the name is empty, the code reverts to wildcard behavior, which is the vulnerable pre-fix state. However, the YAML schema enforces non-empty names at load time, so this edge case is unreachable through the normal builder flow—though it remains available for direct API callers.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5109110Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5110035Z   Now I'm structuring the final JSON response according to the schema requirements, which specifies the persona, active status, findings array, and summary fields. 2026-06-18T19:20:40.5111021Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5111820Z   The second nit concerns the lack of test coverage for package names containing regex special characters like dots, though the escaping logic itself works correctly. Both are minor issues that don't block the security improvements in this fix.

2026-06-18T19:20:40.5152675Z [259] ◐ Now I have a comprehensive picture of the PR and the codebase. Let me analyze and form my architectural review.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5153144Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5153351Z   **The PR in summary:**
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5153977Z   - One-line fix: `build_tag_regex(pattern)` → `build_tag_regex(pattern, name=entry.name)` in `_resolve_version_range` in `builder.py` line 719.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5154550Z   - This is a straightforward bug fix.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5154846Z   - 134 lines of regression tests added.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5155106Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5155382Z   **Architectural context:**
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5155619Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5156422Z   1. `tag_pattern.py` provides the `build_tag_regex` function. The `name` parameter controls whether `{name}` in the pattern is replaced with a wildcard `[^/]+` (when `name=None`) or with the escaped literal name (when `name=entry.name`).
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5157272Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5157458Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5158042Z   2. Without the `name` parameter, `{name}` becomes a wildcard, causing all packages in the monorepo to match all tags—the root cause of the bug.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5158607Z   3. The fix is correct and minimal.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5158859Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5159137Z   **Consistency observation (from PR body, confirmed in code):**
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5159478Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5160173Z   In `version_resolver.py`, `resolve_version_constraint` takes a different approach by manually substituting `{name}` before calling `build_tag_regex`, whereas this code path was missing that substitution step.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5160934Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5161144Z   **Architectural concerns:**
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5161402Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5163386Z   1. **Inconsistency between the two call sites**: Both `_resolve_version_range` and `resolve_version_constraint` need to scope a tag regex to a specific name, but they use different mechanisms. The PR acknowledges this in the trade-offs section and defers standardization. The manual `str.replace` approach means that if `build_tag_regex` ever adds special handling for the `name` field—like error cases, logging, or fallback logic—the `str.replace` path would silently bypass it. The `name=` kwarg is the intended API and should be the standard.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5165099Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5165280Z   2. **
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5165458Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5166335Z   `parse_tag_version` has a redundant guard**: The ternary that checks whether to pass `name=` to `build_tag_regex` is unnecessary since `build_tag_regex` already handles the case where `name=None` and the placeholder is present. This isn't a new issue introduced by the PR, though.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5167333Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5167718Z   3. The fix itself is solid and well-tested with four regre... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 17011 chars]
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5168087Z

2026-06-18T19:20:40.5185817Z

2026-06-18T19:20:40.5187350Z 
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5187943Z [268] ◆ Good — I have all the context I need. The sole remaining `build_tag_regex(pinned_pattern)` bypass is confirmed at `version_resolver.py:81`. Let me produce the final JSON.
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5188566Z   
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5188746Z   ```json
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5188922Z   {
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5189129Z     "persona": "python-architect",
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5189384Z     "active": true,
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5190017Z     "summary": "Correct one-line fix with strong regression suite; recommend standardising build_tag_regex(name=) in version_resolver.py to close the remaining divergence window.",
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5190677Z     "findings": [
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5190888Z       {
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5191083Z         "severity": "recommended",
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5191739Z         "summary": "version_resolver.resolve_version_constraint bypasses the name= kwarg via manual str.replace, creating a silent divergence risk",
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5195532Z         "rationale": "resolve_version_constraint (version_resolver.py:80-81) does pinned_pattern = tag_pattern.replace('{name}', plugin_name) then calls build_tag_regex(pinned_pattern) with no name= arg. This pre-substitutes the name manually rather than routing through the canonical kwarg the PR just proved is correct. If build_tag_regex ever evolves its name handling -- empty-name guard, re.escape edge cases, error paths -- this call site silently diverges without a test to catch it. All other external callers (tag_pattern.py:138, tag_pattern.py:164) already use the name= kwarg form. The PR notes the inconsistency in its Trade-offs section and defers it; given the fix proves name= is the right idiom and the change is one line in one function, standardising now costs less than a deferred cleanup and eliminates the divergence window permanently.\n\nDesign patterns -- Used in this PR: none -- straight-line procedural bug fix (missing argument), appropriate for this scope. Pragmatic suggestion: none -- the current shape is the simplest correct design at this scope; no abstraction would pay off here.",
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5199262Z         "file": "src/apm_cli/marketplace/version_resolver.py",
2026-06-18T19:20:40.5199578Z         "line": 80,

Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. 2026-06-18T19:20:40.7531662Z {"timestamp":"2026-06-18T18:56:52.847Z","event":"rpc_request","_schema":"rpc-message/v2","direction":"OUT","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"method":"get","owner":"microsoft","pullNumber":"1841","repo":"apm"},"name":"pull_request_read"}}}
2026-06-18T19:20:40.7545080Z {"timestamp":"2026-06-18T18:56:53.739Z","event":"rpc_response","_schema":"rpc-message/v2","direction":"IN","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":1841,\"title\":\"Fix {name} placeholder ignored in apm pack marketplace version resolution (#1822)\",\"body\":\"## TL;DR\\n\\n`apm pack` was pinning every monorepo package to the same (highest global) version tag instead of each package\\u0026#39;s own tag. One missing argument caused `{name}` to become a wildcard.\\n\\n## Problem (WHY)\\n\\nWhen `_resolve_version_range` in `builder.py` builds the tag-matching regex:\\n\\n```python\\n# Before\\ntag_rx = build_tag_regex(pattern)\\n```\\n\\nThe `name` keyword argument was omitted. `build_tag_regex` handles a missing `name` by substituting `[^/]+` (wildcard) for `{name}` in the compiled regex. In a monorepo marketplace with pattern `{name}-v{version}`, this means:\\n\\n- `pkg-a-v1.0.0` matched (wildcard covers `pkg-a`)\\n- `pkg-b-v2.0.0` also matched (wildcard covers `pkg-b`)\\n\\nBoth packages competed in the same candidate pool, so the one with the highest semver won -- `pkg-b-v2.0.0` for **both** entries. Closes #1822.\\n\\n## Approach (WHAT)\\n\\nPass `name=entry.name` to `build_tag_regex`:\\n\\n```python\\n# After\\ntag_rx = build_tag_regex(pattern, name=entry.name)\\n```\\n\\n`build_tag_regex` already supports this argument (`re.escape(name)` substitution, implemented at `tag_pattern.py:124`). It is correctly used in `infer_tag_pattern`, `parse_tag_version`, and `version_resolver.resolve_version_constraint`. The omission in `_resolve_version_range` was the sole source of the bug.\\n\\n## Implementation (HOW)\\n\\n```diff\\n-        tag_rx = build_tag_regex(pattern)\\n+        tag_rx = build_tag_regex(pattern, name=entry.name)\\n```\\n\\nOne line, one file. `build_tag_regex` is fully defensive: patterns without `{name}` are unaffected (the guard `_PLACEHOLDER_NAME in pattern and name` short-circuits). Empty names and names with special chars are safe (`re.escape`).\\n\\n## Diagrams\\n\\n```mermaid\\nflowchart TD\\n    A[\\u0026#34;_resolve_version_range(entry, ...)\\u0026#34;] --\\u0026gt; B[\\u0026#34;pattern = entry.tag_pattern or yml.build.tag_pattern\\u0026#34;]\\n    B --\\u0026gt; C{\\u0026#34;pattern contains {name}?\\u0026#34;}\\n    C --\\u0026gt;|\\u0026#34;Before fix (no name arg)\\u0026#34;| D[\\u0026#34;build_tag_regex(pattern)\\\\n{name} -\\u0026gt; [^/]+ wildcard\\u0026#34;]\\n    C --\\u0026gt;|\\u0026#34;After fix\\u0026#34;| E[\\u0026#34;build_tag_regex(pattern, name=entry.name)\\\\n{name} -\\u0026gt; re.escape(entry.name)\\u0026#34;]\\n    D --\\u0026gt; F[\\u0026#34;All packages\\u0026#39; tags match\\\\n-\\u0026gt; highest global version wins\\u0026#34;]\\n    E --\\u0026gt; G[\\u0026#34;Only this package\\u0026#39;s tags match\\\\n-\\u0026gt; correct per-package resolution\\u0026#34;]\\n```\\n\\n## Trade-offs\\n\\n- `version_resolver.resolve_version_constraint` uses a different but equivalent pattern (external `str.replace` before calling `build_tag_regex`). Both patterns are correct; standardizing on the `name=` kwarg form is deferred as a clean-up (no functional impact).\\n\\n## Validation evidence\\n\\nFull lint chain passes:\\n```\\nruff check src/ tests/  -- All checks passed\\nruff format --check     -- 1294 files already formatted\\npylint R0801            -- 10.00/10\\nlint-auth-signals.sh    -- [+] auth-signal lint clean\\n```\\n\\nNew regression tests (all passing):\\n```\\nTestMonorepoNameScopedTagPattern::test_two_packages_resolve_independently        PASSED\\nTestMonorepoNameScopedTagPattern::test_two_packages_double_dash_pattern          PASSED\\nTestMonorepoNameScopedTagPattern::test_per_entry_tag_pattern_scopes_to_name      PASSED\\nTestMonorepoNameScopedTagPattern::test_no_match_when_only_other_package_tags_exist PASSED\\n```\\n\\nFull `test_builder.py`: 138 passed.\\n\\n## How to test\\n\\n```bash\\n# Run the regression suite\\nuv run --extra dev pytest tests/unit/marketplace/test_builder.py::TestMonorepoNameScopedTagPattern -v\\n\\n# Run full builder + version resolver tests\\nuv run --extra dev pytest tests/unit/marketplace/test_builder.py tests/unit/marketplace/test_version_resolver.py -v\\n```\",\"state\":\"open\",\"draft\":false,\"merged\":false,\"mergeable_state\":\"blocked\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/microsoft/apm/pull/1841\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"sergio-sisternes-epam\",\"id\":207026618,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/sergio-sisternes-epam\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/207026618?v=4\"},\"labels\":[\"panel-review\"],\"requested_revie
